title: Density
summary: Defines conformal densities in two equivalent forms: scale-dependent representatives with a transformation law and homogeneous functions on the conformal metric bundle.

mathematicalContent.heading: Weight and scale-change law
mathematicalContent.body: A weight is encoded by a multiplicative character χ of the positive reals. A scale-dependent density assigns a function d_s on M to every scale s. If s₁ is changed to s₂, the representative is multiplied by χ evaluated on the relative scale factor λ_{s₁,s₂}.

mathematicalContent.heading: Equivariant description
mathematicalContent.body: The same density can be viewed as a function F on the conformal metric bundle G. Its value is homogeneous under vertical dilation. Restricting F to a scale gives d_s, while a scale-dependent density reconstructs F by measuring a bundle point relative to any reference scale.

mathematicalContent.heading: Notation from header.tex
mathematicalContent.body: The header shortcut R is expanded as the real numbers, while conformal and metric objects are written with standard operator and calligraphic notation. This avoids passing private TeX commands or Lean tokens such as →*, ⟨x,g⟩, and proof terms to KaTeX.

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: A weight is a monoid homomorphism from positive rescalings to the coefficient monoid α. For the usual real weight w one may take χ(r)=r^w.
correspondence.leanType: abbrev Weight (α : Type w) [Monoid α] := PosReal →* α

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Rescaling transports a representative f in scale s₁ to scale s₂ by multiplying pointwise by the character of the relative factor.
correspondence.leanType: noncomputable def rescale (χ : Weight α) (s₁ s₂ : Conf.Scale) (f : M → α) : M → α

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: A scale-dependent density is a family of representatives d_s:M→α, one for every scale, satisfying the conformal transformation law between any two scales.
correspondence.leanType: structure Density (χ : Weight α) where val : Conf.Scale → M → α; compat : ∀ s₁ s₂ : Conf.Scale, val s₂ = rescale Conf χ s₁ s₂ (val s₁)

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: The representative of d in a chosen scale s is the function d_s on M.
correspondence.leanType: def repr (d : Density (Conf := Conf) χ) (s : Conf.Scale) : M → α := d s

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: An equivariant density is a function F from the conformal metric bundle G to α that is homogeneous with character χ under positive vertical dilation.
correspondence.leanType: structure Equivariant.Density (χ : Weight α) where toFun : Conf.MetricBundle → α; equivariant : ∀ (r : PosReal) (u : Conf.MetricBundle), toFun (Conf.dilateBundle r u) = χ r * toFun u

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Changing the reference scale factors the fiber coordinate into the coordinate relative to s₂ times the transition factor from s₁ to s₂.
correspondence.leanType: theorem scaleFactor_eq_mul_relativeFactor (s₁ s₂ : Conf.Scale) {x : M} (g : Conf.MetricFiber x) : Conf.scaleFactor s₁ g = Conf.scaleFactor s₂ g * Conf.relativeFactor s₁ s₂ x

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Using a reference scale s₀, a scale-dependent density d produces a homogeneous function on G by multiplying d_{s₀}(x) by the character of the fiber coordinate relative to s₀.
correspondence.leanType: noncomputable def toEquivariantUsing (d : Density (Conf := Conf) χ) (s₀ : Conf.Scale) : Equivariant.Density (Conf := Conf) χ

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: The homogeneous function reconstructed from d does not depend on which reference scale is used.
correspondence.leanType: theorem toEquivariantUsing_independent (d : Density (Conf := Conf) χ) (s₀ s₁ : Conf.Scale) : toEquivariantUsing (Conf := Conf) d s₀ = toEquivariantUsing (Conf := Conf) d s₁

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: When at least one global scale exists, toEquivariant chooses one and applies the reference-scale construction. Independence proves that the result is intrinsic.
correspondence.leanType: noncomputable def toEquivariant (d : Density (Conf := Conf) χ) [Nonempty Conf.Scale] : Equivariant.Density (Conf := Conf) χ

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Because the construction is independent of the reference, its value can be computed using any selected global scale s₀.
correspondence.leanType: theorem toEquivariant_apply (d : Density (Conf := Conf) χ) [Nonempty Conf.Scale] (s₀ : Conf.Scale) (x : M) (g : Conf.MetricFiber x) : toEquivariant (Conf := Conf) d ⟨x, g⟩ = χ (Conf.scaleFactor s₀ g) * d s₀ x

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: An equivariant density F yields a scale-dependent family by restricting F to the section of G determined by each scale.
correspondence.leanType: def toScaleDependent (d : Equivariant.Density (Conf := Conf) χ) : ConformalStructure.Density (Conf := Conf) χ

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Once a global scale exists, restriction to scale sections and homogeneous reconstruction are inverse equivalences between the two definitions of a conformal density.
correspondence.leanType: noncomputable def equivScaleDependentEquiv [Nonempty Conf.Scale] : ConformalStructure.Equivariant.Density (Conf := Conf) χ ≃ ConformalStructure.Density (Conf := Conf) χ

references.locator: Chapters 2–3, homogeneous functions and conformal densities
references.note: Background for representing conformal densities by homogeneous functions on the metric bundle.

limitations: The equivalence is proved for an abstract coefficient commutative monoid and assumes that a global scale exists. The file formalizes the algebraic scale-change law; it does not yet construct smooth density bundles or their analytic regularity.
